sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Severe acid reflux symptoms, including heartburn and throat irritation.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a several-year history of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), with symptoms worsening over the past few years. He currently takes 40 mg of omeprazole twice daily, but reports that it is not adequately controlling his symptoms, experiencing heartburn despite medication. He identifies spicy foods, carbonated beverages, citrus fruits, and tomato-based foods as triggers. He also notes significant stress and anxiety, which he believes contribute to his symptoms. He has had a baby at one month old and had surgery for intestinal twisting when he was one month old. He has not had an upper endoscopy in the last 10 years. He has previously tried omeprazole and pantoprazole, but has not had sustained relief.
Findings
Physical exam is unremarkable. No signs of acute distress. Abdominal exam is normal. Vital signs are within normal limits. Patient reports no difficulty swallowing.
Assessment
Clinical presentation is consistent with poorly controlled GERD. Further investigation is warranted to rule out underlying causes.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: Prescribe nexium 40 mg twice daily. Schedule an upper endoscopy to evaluate for esophageal abnormalities. Recommend dietary modifications, including avoiding trigger foods and stress management techniques. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess response to treatment and discuss further management options. Insert procedure paragraph in the plan.
